author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Epistle LXX.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Cyprian, Liberalis, Caldonius, etc., to their brethren Januarius, etc. Greeting.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: When we were together in council, dearest brethren, we read the letter which you addressed to us respecting those who are thought to be baptized by heretics and schismatics, whether, when they come to the one true Catholic Church, they ought to be baptized.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Wherein, although ye yourselves also hold the Catholic rule in its truth and fixedness, yet since, out of our mutual affection, ye have thought good to consult us, we deliver not our sentence as though new but, by a kindred harmony, we unite with you in that long since settled by our predecessors, and observed by us; thinking, namely, and holding for certain, that no one can be baptized without the Church, in that there is one Baptism appointed in the holy Church, and it is written, the Lord himself speaking, “They have forsaken me, the Fountain of living water, and hewed them out broken cisterns that can hold no water.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Again, holy Scripture admonishes us, and says, “Keep thee from the strange water, and drink not from a fountain of strange water.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: The water then must first be cleansed and sanctified by the priest, that it may be able, by Baptism therein, to wash away the sins of the baptized, for the Lord says by the prophet Ezekiel, “Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be cleansed from all your filthiness, and from all your idols will I cleanse you; a new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But how can he cleanse and sanctify the water, who is himself unclean, and with whom the Spirit is not? whereas the Lord says in Numbers, “And whatsoever the unclean person toucheth shall be unclean.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Or how can he that baptizeth give remission of sins to another, who cannot himself free himself from his own sins, out of the Church?

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Moreover, the very interrogatory which is put in Baptism, is a witness of the truth.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: For when we say, “Dost thou believe in eternal life, and remission of sins through the holy Church?” we mean, that remission of sins is not given, except in the Church; but that, with heretics, where the Church is not, sins cannot be remitted.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: They, therefore, who claim that heretics can baptize, let them either change the interrogatory, or maintain the truth; unless indeed they ascribe a Church also to those who they contend have Baptism.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Anointed also must he of necessity be, who is baptized, that having received the chrism—that is, unction, he may be the anointed of God, and have within him the grace of Christ.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Moreover, it is the Eucharist through which the baptized are anointed, the oil sanctified on the altar.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But he cannot sanctify the creature of oil, who has neither altar nor church.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Whence neither can the spiritual unction be with heretics, since it is acknowledged that the oil cannot be sanctified nor the Eucharist celebrated among them.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But we ought to know and remember that it is written, “Let not the oil of a sinner anoint my head;” which the Holy Ghost forewarned in the Psalms, lest any, quitting the track, and wandering out of the path of truth, be anointed by heretics and adversaries of Christ.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Moreover, when baptized, what kind of prayer can a profane priest and a sinner offer? in that it is written, “God heareth not a sinner; but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But who can give what himself hath not? or how can he perform spiritual acts, who hath himself lost the Holy Spirit?

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Wherefore he is to be baptized and received, who comes uninitiated to the Church, that within he may be hallowed through the holy; for it is written, “Be ye holy, for I am holy, saith the Lord.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: So that he who has been seduced into error and washed without should, in the true Baptism of the Church, put off this very thing also; that he, a man coming to God, while seeking for a priest, fell, through the deceit of error, upon one profane.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But to acknowledge any case where they have baptized, is to approve the baptism of heretics and schismatics.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: For neither can part of what they do be void and part avail.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: If he could baptize, he could also give the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: But if he cannot give the Holy Ghost because, being set without, he is not with the Holy Ghost, neither can he baptize any that cometh: for that there is both one Baptism, and one Holy Ghost, and one Church, founded by Christ the Lord upon Peter, through an original and principle of unity; so it results, that since all among them is void and false, nothing that they have done ought to be approved by us.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: For what can be ratified and confirmed by God, which they do whom the Lord calls his enemies and adversaries, propounding in his Gospel, “He that is not with me, is against me; and he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: And the blessed Apostle John also, keeping the commandments and precepts of the Lord, has written in his Epistle, “Ye have heard that Antichrist shall come; even now are there many Antichrists, whereby we know that it is the last time.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: They went out from us, but were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would no doubt have continued with us.”

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Whence we, too, ought to infer and consider, whether they who are the adversaries of the Lord, and are called Antichrists, can give the grace of Christ.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: Wherefore we who are with the Lord, and who hold the unity of the Lord, and according to this vouchsafement administer his priesthood in the Church, ought to repudiate and reject and account as profane, whatever his adversaries and Antichrists do; and to those who, coming from error and wickedness, acknowledge the true faith of the one Church, we should impart the reality of unity and faith by all the sacraments of Divine grace.

verses.book: The Council of Carthage held under Cyprian.
verses.text: We bid you, dearest brethren, ever heartily farewell.
